stem: At what \( x \) does the curve \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+([[b]])x \) have a tangent line of slope \( [[m]] \)?
Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x^{3} + ([[b]])x^{2} \). Which of the following is a value of \( x \) at which the curve \( y=f(x) \) has a tangent line of slope \( [[m]] \)?
solution: \( f'(x) = 3([[a]])x^{2} + 2([[b]])x \). Setting \( f'(x)=[[m]] \) gives \( 3([[a]])x^{2}+2([[b]])x-([[m]])=0 \), which factors with roots \( x=[[r1]] \) and \( x=[[r2]] \); \( [[answer]] \) is one of them.
distractor_rationales: This is the negative of one correct root.
This is the negative of the other correct root.
This is the sum of the two roots, not a root itself.
This is the product of the two roots, not a root itself.
This is off by one from a correct root.
This is off by one from the other correct root.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x^{3} + ([[b]])x^{2} + ([[c]])x \). Which of the following is a value of \( x \) at which the curve \( y=f(x) \) has a horizontal tangent line?
solution: A horizontal tangent occurs where \( f'(x)=0 \). Since \( f'(x) = 3([[a]])x^{2}+2([[b]])x+([[c]]) \), setting this to \( 0 \) gives roots \( x=[[r1]] \) and \( x=[[r2]] \); \( [[answer]] \) is one of them.
distractor_rationales: This is the negative of one correct root.
This is the negative of the other correct root.
This is the sum of the two roots, not a root itself.
This is the product of the two roots, not a root itself.
This is off by one from a correct root.
This is off by one from the other correct root.

stem: The curve \( y = \sqrt[3]{[[m]]x + ([[n]])} + ([[k]]) \) has a vertical tangent line at \( x = ? \)
solution: The derivative of \( \sqrt[3]{[[m]]x+([[n]])} \) blows up (is undefined) exactly where the radicand \( [[m]]x+([[n]]) \) equals \( 0 \), i.e. at \( x = -\dfrac{[[n]]}{[[m]]} = [[answer]] \); the vertical shift by \( [[k]] \) does not affect where this happens.
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign.
This inverts the ratio of coefficients.
This inverts the ratio and has the wrong sign.
This subtracts the coefficients instead of dividing them.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: At what value of \( x \) is the tangent line to the curve \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+([[b]])x \) parallel to the line \( y=[[p]]x+([[q]]) \)?
distractor_rationales: This adds instead of subtracts \( b \) from the line's slope.
This forgets the factor of \( 2 \) from differentiating \( ax^{2} \).
This drops \( b \) entirely from the slope condition.
This forgets to divide by \( 2a \) at all.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: At what value of \( x \) is the tangent line to the curve \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+([[b]])x \) perpendicular to the line \( y=[[p]]x+([[q]]) \)?
solution: A line \( y=[[p]]x+([[q]]) \) has slope \( [[p]] \), so a perpendicular tangent has slope \( -\frac{1}{[[p]]} \). Setting \( y'=2[[a]]x+([[b]])=-\frac{1}{[[p]]} \) gives \( x=\dfrac{-\frac{1}{[[p]]}-([[b]])}{2[[a]]}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign on the reciprocal slope.
This uses \( -p \) instead of the negative reciprocal \( -\frac{1}{p} \).
This has the wrong sign on \( b \).
This uses the parallel-line condition instead of the perpendicular one.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: From the external point \( ([[p]], [[q]]) \), which does not lie on the curve \( y=[[a]]x^{2} \), two tangent lines are drawn to the curve. Which of the following is the \( x \)-coordinate of one of the two points of tangency?
solution: The tangent to \( y=[[a]]x^{2} \) at \( (t, [[a]]t^{2}) \) is \( y = 2([[a]])t\,x - ([[a]])t^{2} \). Requiring this to pass through \( ([[p]],[[q]]) \) gives \( ([[a]])t^{2} - 2([[a]])([[p]])t + ([[q]]) = 0 \), whose roots are \( t=[[t1]] \) and \( t=[[t2]] \); \( [[answer]] \) is one of them.
distractor_rationales: This is the external point's own \( x \)-coordinate, not a point of tangency.
This is the negative of one correct tangency point.
This is the negative of the other correct tangency point.
This is the sum of the two tangency points, not a point of tangency itself.
This is off by one from a correct tangency point.
This is off by one from the other correct tangency point.

stem: For what value of \( k \) does the curve \( y = kx^{2} + ([[b]])x \) have a tangent line of slope \( [[m]] \) at the point where \( x=[[x0]] \)?
distractor_rationales: This adds instead of subtracts \( b \).
This forgets the factor of \( 2 \) from differentiating \( kx^{2} \).
This drops \( b \) entirely from the slope condition.
This forgets to divide by \( 2x_0 \) at all.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: At what value of \( x \) do the curves \( y=[[a]]x^{2}+([[b]])x \) and \( y=[[c]]x^{2}+([[d]])x \) have parallel tangent lines?
solution: Parallel tangent lines require equal slopes: \( 2([[a]])x+([[b]]) = 2([[c]])x+([[d]]) \). Solving gives \( x = \dfrac{([[d]])-([[b]])}{2(([[a]])-([[c]]))} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the numerator's sign flipped.
This adds instead of subtracts the leading coefficients in the denominator.
This forgets the factor of \( 2 \) in the denominator.
This is missing the denominator's factor of \( 2(a-c) \) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
